patches.find: without any explanation of how a flat disc produces this observation. On a flat disc 20,015 km in radius, there is no spherical liquid core to refract P-waves — a flat disc would produce simple geometric attenuation with distance, not a sharp angular shadow zone with PKP re-emergence beyond 140°. The dome offers no flat-disc mechanism that predicts these specific cutoff angles. The IRIS Global Seismographic Network (143 stations) and GEOSCOPE (30 stations) have mapped this shadow zone consistently for decades; P-wave arrival times, amplitudes, and slownesses all fit a spherical Earth model to better than 1% precision across thousands of earthquake-station pairs. Notably, the dome's own source files contain no discussion of P-wave shadow zones at any of its seven pages — WIN-064 was added in V51.0 without any explanation of how a flat disc produces this observation.
patches.replace: without any explanation of how a flat disc produces this observation.
patches.rationale: Remove verbatim duplicate block at end of WIN-064 detail_evidence. "On a flat disc 20,015 km in radius, there is no spherical liquid core..." block appears twice. Curmudgeon Cycle 2 flagged this as an embarrassing copy-paste error.

patches.find: appeared after extensive site development work including V13 coordinate system revisions. Polaris's declination (~89.26°), proper motion, and altitude-latitude relationship have been published in standard star catalogs (FK5, Hipparcos, Tycho-2) for decades. Registering a 'prediction' about well-known stellar data is retrodiction, not prospective prediction — the dome had access to the expected result before formulating the claim. Notably, the dome's broader prediction 'Polaris altitude matches latitude' is listed as FALSIFIED on the dome's own site (2026-03-15) — the model cannot simultaneously claim both falsification and confirmation of related predictions.
patches.rationale: Remove duplicate PRED-NEW-001 retrodiction paragraph from WIN-065. The argument appears twice with slightly different wording. Removing the expanded second version leaves the concise first version intact.

patches.find: not systematic at all — it depends on the time of observation during Polaris’s diurnal circle.
patches.replace: not systematic at all — it depends on the time of observation during Polaris’s diurnal circle. Of 24 cities in the dome’s own coordinate table, 23 match WGS84 latitude to within ±0.05°, consistent with the globe model. The dome’s own table lists Oslo Polaris elevation at 59.90° (WGS84: 59.91°N), showing essentially zero excess — directly contradicting PRED-NEW-001’s prediction of a systematic multi-degree elevation excess at high latitudes.
patches.rationale: Add Oslo counterargument: dome own coordinate table shows 23 of 24 cities at essentially zero Polaris excess (Oslo: 59.90° vs WGS84 59.91°N), directly contradicting PRED-NEW-001's prediction of a systematic multi-degree excess. Curmudgeon Cycle 2 identified this as a missing killer argument.
Fix velocity rounding: 40,075/0.08484 = 472,360 km/s (not 472,400), and the percentage difference is 0.24% (not 0.25%). Minor rounding error in a precision-focused argument.
Fix P-wave shadow zone angular range in part7 from "140-103° from epicenter" (reversed, wrong inner boundary) to standard "104°-140°", consistent with WIN-064 and seismology literature.
